seoTitle: Pleasant Park vs Plympton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pleasant Park and Plympton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pleasant Park vs Plympton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pleasant Park and Plympton.
heading: Pleasant Park vs Plympton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pleasant Park, South Australia and Plympton, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pleasant Park has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Plympton (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pleasant Park or Plympton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pleasant Park has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Plympton scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pleasant Park scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Plympton scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pleasant Park or Plympton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pleasant Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
